title: Dog Thick Yellow Eye Discharge: Causes and Triage Advice
meta_desc: Learn why your dog has thick yellow eye discharge. Discover when to see a vet, urgency levels, and why triage photos are essential for diagnosis.
content_html:
What is Thick Yellow Eye Discharge?

Thick yellow or green discharge in dogs, often referred to as mucopurulent discharge, is usually a sign that the body is fighting an infection or significant irritation. Unlike clear tearing, this discharge contains white blood cells and cellular debris. Common causes include bacterial conjunctivitis, corneal ulcers, or Keratoconjunctivitis Sicca (Dry Eye), where the eye does not produce enough natural tears to stay lubricated.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for thick yellow eye discharge is considered Medium. While it may not always require an emergency hospital visit in the middle of the night, your dog should be seen by a veterinarian within 24 hours. Eye conditions can deteriorate rapidly; if the discharge is accompanied by squinting, cloudiness of the eye, or if your dog is pawing at their face, you should seek care as soon as possible to prevent permanent vision loss.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear, well-lit photo of your dog's eye can be incredibly helpful for veterinary triage. A photo allows a professional to see the exact color and consistency of the discharge, the degree of redness in the sclera (the white of the eye), and whether the eyelid is swollen. This information helps the veterinary team determine how quickly your pet needs to be squeezed into the schedule and whether the condition appears to be a surface infection or a more serious internal eye issue.

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops on my dog's yellow discharge?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human eye drops or medications on your dog without a veterinarian's approval. Some human medications contain ingredients that can cause severe irritation or even blindness in dogs if an underlying corneal ulcer is present.

faqs.question: How should I clean the discharge while waiting for the vet?
faqs.answer: You can gently wipe away the discharge using a soft cloth or cotton ball dampened with lukewarm water or sterile saline solution. Always wipe away from the eye and use a fresh cloth for each eye to prevent spreading potential infection.

faqs.question: Is yellow eye discharge contagious to other pets?
faqs.answer: It depends on the cause. If the discharge is due to a bacterial or viral infection, it can potentially be spread to other dogs. It is best to keep the affected dog separated from other pets until a veterinarian has provided a diagnosis.
